questions.question: OneLake is built on which underlying storage technology?

options.A: Azure Blob Storage (Gen1)
options.B: Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 (ADLS Gen2)
options.C: Azure SQL Database

questions.explanation: OneLake is built on Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 (ADLS Gen2) and supports various formats including Delta, Parquet, CSV, and JSON.

questions.question: Which three administrator roles may be involved in enabling Microsoft Fabric for an organization?

options.A: Fabric administrator, Power Platform administrator, and Global administrator
options.B: Database administrator, Network administrator, and Security administrator
options.C: Workspace admin, Contributor, and Viewer
options.D: Capacity admin, Tenant admin, and Domain admin

questions.explanation: Enabling Fabric may involve the Fabric administrator (manages Fabric settings), Power Platform administrator (oversees Power Platform services including Fabric), and Global administrator (has implicit Fabric admin rights through organization-wide permissions).

questions.question: What is the core item of the Fabric IQ workload?

options.A: The lakehouse
options.B: The ontology
options.C: The pipeline

questions.explanation: Fabric IQ's core item is the ontology, which defines business concepts, relationships, and rules so that AI agents can reason across domains using consistent business language rather than raw table schemas.

questions.question: An organization currently moves and copies datasets between separate storage systems for each analytics tool, causing duplication and governance gaps. Which Fabric capability most directly addresses this by unifying data across regions and clouds without moving or duplicating it?

options.A: Git integration in workspaces
options.B: OneLake's centralized data storage architecture
options.C: Spark workload settings

questions.explanation: OneLake is Fabric's centralized data storage architecture that enables collaboration by eliminating the need to move or copy data between systems. It unifies data across regions and clouds into a single logical lake without moving or duplicating it.

questions.question: A data team needs to access data stored in an external Amazon S3 bucket from within Fabric, but must avoid copying the data and must keep it in sync with the source. Which OneLake feature should they use?

options.A: A scheduled Data Factory copy pipeline
options.B: A shortcut
options.C: A Direct Lake semantic model

questions.explanation: Shortcuts are references to external data sources such as Amazon S3 that allow access to existing data without copying it, ensuring data consistency and enabling Fabric to stay in sync with the source.

questions.question: An IT lead wants to ensure that only authorized members of a specific project team can view or modify a set of related lakehouses and reports, while keeping resources cleanly separated from other projects. What should they use to achieve this separation and access control?

options.A: A single shared OneLake folder for all projects
options.B: Separate workspaces, each with its own set of permissions
options.C: Tenant-wide enabling of Fabric

questions.explanation: Workspaces provide a clear separation of resources and each workspace has its own set of permissions, ensuring that only authorized users can view or modify its contents while supporting team collaboration with strict access control.

questions.question: A business analyst with no engineering background needs to find a curated dataset that has already been prepared and shared, then quickly build a report. Which combination of Fabric capabilities best supports this self-service need?

options.A: Use Notebooks with Spark to query raw files, then export to CSV
options.B: Discover curated datasets through the OneLake catalog and use Power BI templates
options.C: Configure Spark workload settings and write PySpark transformations
options.D: Manually request data engineers to email extracts

questions.explanation: Low-to-no-code users and citizen developers can discover curated datasets through the OneLake catalog and use Power BI templates to quickly create reports and dashboards, reducing reliance on data engineers.

questions.question: A data analyst is frustrated by repeatedly performing downstream transformations before building Power BI reports. Which Fabric capability lets them connect directly to data in OneLake and reduce the need for those downstream transformations?

options.A: Direct Lake mode
options.B: Import mode with scheduled refresh
options.C: Shortcuts to Amazon S3

questions.explanation: Data analysts can transform data upstream using dataflows and connect directly to OneLake with Direct Lake mode, reducing the need for downstream transformations and enabling more efficient interactive reporting in Power BI.

questions.question: A data engineer needs to automate an ingest-transform-load workflow that runs on a schedule, loading data into a lakehouse. Which Fabric capability should they primarily use?

options.A: Power BI templates
options.B: Pipelines
options.C: Sensitivity labels

questions.explanation: Data engineers can ingest, transform, and load data directly into OneLake using Pipelines, which automate workflows and support scheduling. They store data in lakehouses using Delta-Parquet format.

questions.question: A data scientist wants to build and test machine learning models using Python and Spark, store data in a lakehouse, and operationalize the models. Which integration does Fabric provide to help deploy those models?

options.A: Integration with Azure Machine Learning
options.B: Integration with Amazon SageMaker
options.C: Integration with the Power BI gateway

questions.explanation: Data scientists can use integrated notebooks with Python and Spark to build and test ML models, store and access data in lakehouses, and integrate with Azure Machine Learning to operationalize and deploy models.

questions.question: An analytics engineer is asked to bridge the gap between raw engineered data and business reporting by ensuring data quality and enabling self-service analytics. Which two activities best describe this role in Fabric?

options.A: Configuring tenant settings and managing gateways
options.B: Curating data assets in lakehouses and creating semantic models in Power BI
options.C: Writing streaming queries and monitoring eventstreams
options.D: Signing up for the free Fabric trial and enabling Copilot

questions.explanation: Analytics engineers bridge the gap between data engineering and analysis by curating data assets in lakehouses, ensuring data quality, and enabling self-service analytics. They can create semantic models in Power BI to organize and present data effectively.

questions.question: An organization is not currently using Fabric or Power BI but wants to explore Fabric's features before committing to licensing. What option is available to them?

options.A: They must purchase an F64 capacity first
options.B: They can sign up for a free Fabric trial
options.C: They must migrate from Azure Synapse first
options.D: Fabric cannot be used without an existing Power BI license

questions.explanation: If an organization isn't using Fabric or Power BI today, it can sign up for a free Fabric trial to explore its features.

questions.question: A governance team wants to decentralize data ownership across business domains while still maintaining centralized governance, and avoid requiring users to have direct Azure resource access. Which Fabric architectural capability supports this?

options.A: A data mesh architecture
options.B: Import mode reporting
options.C: A single monolithic lakehouse owned by IT

questions.explanation: Fabric supports a data mesh architecture, allowing decentralized data ownership while maintaining centralized governance. This design eliminates the need for direct Azure resource access, simplifying data workflows.

questions.question: A Fabric administrator needs to automate common administrative tasks and integrate Fabric with other internal systems. Where can they find the tools to do this?

options.A: In Workspace settings under Git integration
options.B: Through the Fabric admin APIs and SDKs in the admin portal
options.C: In the OneLake catalog default categories

questions.explanation: In the admin portal you can access the Fabric admin APIs and SDKs, which can automate common tasks and integrate Fabric with other systems.

questions.question: A business user wants to ask questions about organizational data in natural language across lakehouses, warehouses, and semantic models, receiving answers from structured queries. Which Fabric capability enables this conversational interface?

options.A: Fabric data agents
options.B: Spark workload settings
options.C: Shortcuts

questions.explanation: Fabric data agents let you build conversational interfaces where users ask questions about organizational data in natural language. Agents translate those questions into structured queries across lakehouses, warehouses, and semantic models.

questions.question: An enterprise wants AI agents to reason over its business data in OneLake and Power BI using consistent business language rather than raw table schemas. Which of the three IQ workloads is designed for this?

options.A: Work IQ
options.B: Foundry IQ
options.C: Fabric IQ

questions.explanation: Fabric IQ models business data (ontologies, semantic models, and graphs) so agents can reason over analytics in OneLake and Power BI using consistent business language.

questions.question: An organization wants agents to access permission-aware enterprise knowledge spanning structured and unstructured data across Azure, SharePoint, OneLake, and the web. Which IQ workload best fits this requirement?

options.A: Fabric IQ
options.B: Foundry IQ
options.C: Work IQ

questions.explanation: Foundry IQ connects structured and unstructured data across Azure, SharePoint, OneLake, and the web so agents can access permission-aware enterprise knowledge.

questions.question: A leadership team wants agents to understand how the organization actually operates by capturing collaboration signals from documents, meetings, chats, and workflows. Which IQ workload addresses this?

options.A: Fabric IQ
options.B: Foundry IQ
options.C: Work IQ

questions.explanation: Work IQ captures collaboration signals from documents, meetings, chats, and workflows, providing agents with insight into how the organization operates.

questions.question: A Fabric administrator wants to enable Fabric only for a specific department rather than the entire organization. Which approach is supported?

options.A: Fabric can only be enabled for the entire organization at once
options.B: Fabric can be enabled for specific Microsoft 365 or Microsoft Entra security groups
options.C: Fabric must be enabled per individual user in Workspace settings
options.D: Fabric is enabled only by purchasing separate licenses per user

questions.explanation: Admins can enable Fabric for the entire organization or for specific Microsoft 365 or Microsoft Entra security groups, and can also delegate this ability to other users at the capacity level.

questions.question: A team complains that the four workspace roles (admin, contributor, member, viewer) grant access too broadly because they apply to all items in a workspace. What does the guidance recommend for more granular control?

options.A: Create a separate tenant for each user
options.B: Use item-level permissions based on business needs
options.C: Disable the workspace and use OneDrive instead
options.D: Remove all roles and rely on sensitivity labels

questions.explanation: The workspace roles apply to all items in a workspace and should be reserved for collaboration. For more granular access control, use item-level permissions based on business needs.

questions.question: A user opens the OneLake catalog and notices they cannot see all of the organization's data items. Why is this expected behavior?

options.A: The catalog only shows items created in the last 30 days
options.B: You only see items that have been shared with you
options.C: The catalog hides all lakehouses by default
options.D: Copilot must be enabled before items appear

questions.explanation: When using the OneLake catalog, you only see items that have been shared with you. You can narrow results by workspaces or domains, explore default categories, and filter by keyword or item type.

questions.question: An organization wants the same governed data that powers its reports and dashboards to also be available to Copilot and data agents without building separate data preparation pipelines. What property of Fabric makes this possible?

options.A: All Fabric workloads store data in OneLake using an open format
options.B: Each workload maintains its own private copy of the data
options.C: Copilot only works with data exported to CSV
options.D: AI capabilities require a separate non-OneLake data store

questions.explanation: Because all Fabric workloads store data in OneLake using an open format, AI capabilities like Copilot and data agents can access the same governed data as reports and dashboards without separate data preparation pipelines.

questions.question: A platform team wants to optimize both performance and cost for the compute resources used by their workspace. Which workspace capability lets them do this?

options.A: Configuring compute settings such as Spark workload settings
options.B: Enabling Copilot
options.C: Creating shortcuts to external data
options.D: Filtering the OneLake catalog by keyword

questions.explanation: Workspaces allow you to manage compute resources; you can optimize performance and cost by configuring compute settings, including Spark workload settings for performance optimization.

questions.question: An organization integrating Fabric notices it brings together capabilities from several existing Microsoft tools. Which set of tools does Fabric integrate into its unified platform?

options.A: Power BI, Azure Synapse Analytics, and Azure Data Factory
options.B: Excel, SharePoint, and Teams
options.C: SQL Server Management Studio, SSIS, and SSRS only
options.D: Visual Studio, GitHub, and Azure DevOps

questions.explanation: Fabric integrates capabilities from existing Microsoft tools like Power BI, Azure Synapse Analytics, and Azure Data Factory into a unified platform.

questions.question: A data team currently struggles because data engineers, analysts, and scientists work in separate systems with extensive coordination, delays, and misinterpretations. According to the content, how does Fabric improve this situation?

options.A: By requiring each role to maintain its own data silo
options.B: By unifying tools into a SaaS platform that removes data silos and lets roles collaborate without duplicating efforts
options.C: By eliminating the data scientist role entirely
options.D: By forcing all transformations to occur downstream in Power BI

questions.explanation: Microsoft Fabric simplifies the analytics development process by unifying tools into a SaaS platform, removing data silos and the need for multiple systems, allowing different roles to collaborate effectively without duplicating efforts.

questions.question: A workspace administrator is reviewing Workspace settings to prepare a new collaborative environment. Which of the following is NOT one of the settings listed as configurable in Workspace settings?

options.A: License type to use Fabric features
options.B: Azure Data Lake Gen2 Storage connection
options.C: Tenant-wide enabling of Copilot for all organizations

questions.explanation: Workspace settings let you configure license type, OneDrive access, Azure Data Lake Gen2 Storage connection, Git integration, and Spark workload settings. Enabling Copilot tenant-wide is done in the Admin portal > Tenant settings, not Workspace settings.

questions.question: An organization wants to understand how each data role contributes to its AI readiness. Which statement best reflects the relationship described between data roles and AI in Fabric?

options.A: Only data scientists contribute to AI readiness
options.B: Data engineers who maintain clean, well-governed data in OneLake build the foundation that Copilot and AI agents rely on, and analytics engineers' semantic models give AI tools business context
options.C: AI capabilities require data to be exported out of OneLake first
options.D: Citizen developers cannot contribute to AI initiatives

questions.explanation: Every role contributes to AI effectiveness: data engineers who maintain clean, well-governed data in OneLake build the foundation that Copilot and AI agents rely on, and analytics engineers who create consistent semantic models give AI tools the business context needed for accurate answers.

questions.question: Contoso Manufacturing operates with disconnected analytics tools: data engineers maintain an on-premises ETL system, analysts copy extracts into separate Power BI files, and data scientists run notebooks in an isolated environment, leading to duplicated data, governance gaps, and slow collaboration. Leadership also wants to launch AI initiatives (Copilot and data agents) that reason over the same governed data, and they want decentralized domain ownership with centralized governance. They plan to adopt Microsoft Fabric. Which combination of Fabric concepts BEST addresses ALL of Contoso's stated needs?

options.A: Use OneLake as the single open-format store so all workloads and AI capabilities share governed data; use workspaces with role-based and item-level permissions for collaboration and security; adopt a data mesh architecture for decentralized ownership with centralized governance; and rely on the unified SaaS platform to let engineers, analysts, and scientists collaborate without silos.
options.B: Keep the on-premises ETL system, export nightly CSVs to each team, and enable Copilot only in Power BI so AI works on the local extracts.
options.C: Give every user Global administrator rights, store each team's data in a separate non-OneLake storage account, and disable governance to speed up collaboration.
options.D: Use a single workspace for the entire company with no permission separation, copy all external data into OneLake nightly, and avoid semantic models to keep things simple.

questions.explanation: OneLake stores all data in a single open format accessible to every workload and to AI capabilities like Copilot and data agents without separate preparation, closing the duplication and governance gaps. Workspaces with the four collaboration roles plus item-level permissions provide secure collaboration. Fabric's data mesh architecture enables decentralized domain ownership while maintaining centralized governance, and the unified SaaS platform removes silos so all data roles collaborate effectively. The other options reintroduce silos, copying, or remove governance, contradicting the content.

questions.question: In a Fabric notebook, a data engineer wants to persist a transformed Spark DataFrame as a managed table in a lakehouse using the format that Fabric's analytical engines use for tabular data. Which line best reflects the recommended approach?

options.A: df.write.format("csv").saveAsTable("sales_curated")
options.B: df.write.format("delta").mode("overwrite").saveAsTable("sales_curated")
options.C: df.write.format("json").save("abfss://external/sales")

questions.explanation: For tabular data, Fabric's analytical engines write data in delta-parquet format, and all engines interact with the format seamlessly. Writing the DataFrame as a Delta table (saveAsTable with format 'delta') aligns with how lakehouse tables are stored in OneLake.

questions.question: A business user asks Copilot in Fabric, 'Show total revenue by region for last quarter.' Copilot is expected to convert this natural-language request into a structured query. Which generated artifact best reflects a Copilot capability described in the content?

options.A: A generated SQL query such as SELECT region, SUM(revenue) FROM sales WHERE quarter = 'Q-last' GROUP BY region
options.B: A manually written shell script that copies CSV files
options.C: A workspace role assignment command

questions.explanation: Copilot in Fabric provides code completion and generation, including generating SQL queries from natural language descriptions. A generated GROUP BY SQL query is a direct example of this capability.

questions.question: For real-time analysis, the content states Copilot can translate natural-language questions into a specific query language. Which generated query language reflects this Real-Time Intelligence capability?

options.A: DAX
options.B: Kusto Query Language (KQL)
options.C: MDX

questions.explanation: Copilot translates questions into Kusto Query Language (KQL) for real-time analysis, in addition to generating SQL queries and providing code suggestions in notebooks.

questions.question: A data engineer wants to create an OneLake shortcut so a lakehouse can reference an existing ADLS Gen2 folder without copying the data. Conceptually, which approach matches the shortcut behavior described in the content?

options.A: Create a shortcut that references the external ADLS Gen2 location, so the data is accessed in place and stays in sync with the source
options.B: Run a nightly pipeline that physically copies all files into a new lakehouse table
options.C: Download the files locally and re-upload them to OneLake
options.D: Convert the external data to CSV and email it to the team

questions.explanation: Shortcuts are references to files or storage locations such as ADLS Gen2 that let you access existing data without copying it, ensuring data consistency and keeping Fabric in sync with the source.

questions.question: An analytics engineer curating a Gold layer table wants self-service consumers to access it via Power BI Direct Lake mode without downstream copies. Conceptually, which sequence aligns with the roles and modes described in the content?

options.A: Curate the table in a lakehouse (Delta-Parquet), build a semantic model, and let analysts connect with Direct Lake mode directly to OneLake
options.B: Export the table to CSV, import it into Power BI Desktop, and schedule daily refreshes
options.C: Store the table in an external S3 bucket and require analysts to download it
options.D: Disable the semantic model and have each analyst write their own transformations downstream

questions.explanation: Analytics engineers curate data assets in lakehouses and create semantic models, and data analysts connect directly to OneLake with Direct Lake mode, reducing the need for downstream transformations. Lakehouse tables use Delta-Parquet format.

questions.question: A team configuring a Fabric data agent in the Fabric IQ workload wants the agent to answer questions using the organization's defined business concepts and relationships rather than raw table schemas. Conceptually, what should the agent connect to as a source?

options.A: The ontology defined in Fabric IQ
options.B: A raw CSV file in OneDrive
options.C: The Admin portal tenant settings

questions.explanation: In the Fabric IQ workload, data agents can connect to your ontology as a source, enabling them to understand and use your business concepts when answering questions, rather than relying on raw table schemas.

questions.question: A data engineer building an automated, scheduled ingestion workflow into a lakehouse must choose the Fabric item that automates workflows and supports scheduling. Which conceptual choice is correct?

options.A: Use a Pipeline to ingest, transform, and load data into OneLake on a schedule
options.B: Use a Power BI report to ingest the source data nightly
options.C: Use the OneLake catalog to schedule transformations
options.D: Use sensitivity labels to trigger the data load

questions.explanation: Data engineers can ingest, transform, and load data directly into OneLake using Pipelines, which automate workflows and support scheduling, storing data in lakehouses using the Delta-Parquet format.
